title: Robust Crumbly Norwegian Fish Casserole
description: A traditional Norwegian dish featuring cod, potatoes, and a crunchy topping, this casserole is a crowd-pleaser, with a bitter and crumbly texture that is sure to satisfy, made with ayurvedic sattvic friendly ingredients and a special ice bath shock step to lock in the flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot of boiling salted water, cook the sliced potatoes until they are slightly tender, then immediately transfer them to an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
In a separate pot, cook the grated carrots until they are tender, then set them aside.
In a large skillet, saute the sliced onions until they are softened and lightly browned.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked potatoes, carrots, onions, and cod fillets, and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and top with the melted butter and rolled oats.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the casserole and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the casserole is hot and bubbly.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the casserole hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
